channel.title: Yael Shechter | Security Research & Binary Exploitation
channel.description: Binary exploitation and vulnerability research writeups, focused on Linux internals and hands-on pwnable.kr challenges.

item.title: pwnable.kr: exynos
item.description: CVE-2012-6422: ARM kernel exploitation via /dev/mem, physical memory scan and credential overwrite (Hacker's Secret)

item.title: pwnable.kr: brainfuck
item.description: GOT overwrite and function chaining in a brainfuck interpreter

item.title: pwnable.kr: horcrux
item.description: ROP chain and buffer overflow exploitation

item.title: pwnable.kr: syscall
item.description: Kernel syscall table manipulation and privilege escalation

item.title: pwnable.kr: ascii_easy
item.description: Buffer overflow with ASCII-only payload constraints

item.title: pwnable.kr: unlink
item.description: Heap exploitation via unlink attack technique

item.title: pwnable.kr: uaf
item.description: C++ use-after-free exploit with vtable hijacking

item.title: pwnable.kr: tiny_easy
item.description: 90-byte binary with heap spraying and ASLR bypass

item.title: pwnable.kr: asm
item.description: Writing shellcode under seccomp sandbox restrictions

item.title: pwnable.kr: bof
item.description: Buffer overflow with stack canary enabled

item.title: pwnable.kr: leg
item.description: ARM assembly reverse engineering with PC register calculations

item.title: pwnable.kr: fd
item.description: File descriptor manipulation and Linux I/O fundamentals
